title: sterculia urophylla
intro: Sterculia urophylla, also known as the elephant ear or elephant tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which have been found to help alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Sterculia urophylla include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and act as an antiseptic agent. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, are often used to treat various ailments, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
